seoTitle: How to Add Status to a Jira Column
metaDescription: Learn how to map an issue status to a column on your Jira board so cards land exactly where your workflow expects them.
intro: Each column on a Jira board is really just a bucket for one or more statuses. If a status isn't mapped to a column, its issues won't show up where you expect. This guide covers how to add a status to a board column correctly.

steps.heading: Open board settings
steps.body: Go to your board and open the settings area where columns are configured.

steps.heading: Go to the columns tab
steps.body: Find the Columns section, which lists your current columns and the statuses mapped to each.

steps.heading: Add the status to a column
steps.body: Drag the unmapped status into the column where you want it to appear, or add a new column for it.

steps.heading: Reorder columns if needed
steps.body: Adjust the column order so the workflow reads left to right in the sequence your team actually follows.

steps.heading: Save and verify on the board
steps.body: Return to the board and confirm issues with that status now appear in the correct column.

faq.question: Why is a status missing from my board entirely?
faq.answer: If a status hasn't been mapped to any column yet, Jira usually flags it in the columns settings so you can assign it.

faq.question: Can multiple statuses share one column?
faq.answer: Yes, a single column can represent several statuses grouped together, such as combining 'In Review' and 'In QA'.

faq.question: Does changing column mapping affect the underlying workflow?
faq.answer: No, column mapping only changes how statuses are displayed on the board, not the workflow's actual transitions.

faq.question: Will existing issues move automatically after remapping?
faq.answer: Yes, once a status is mapped to a new column, any issue with that status will appear there immediately.
